elements.name: Feature announcement banner
elements.notes: In-app banner explaining what the AI feature does and how to opt out. Appears on first login after rollout.

elements.name: User reads AI feature explanation
elements.notes: User encounters the feature description and decides whether to enable it.

elements.name: Contextual onboarding tooltip
elements.notes: Tooltip explaining the first AI recommendation the user sees, with a confidence indicator.

elements.name: User accepts first AI recommendation
elements.notes: Critical action — measures first-use conversion from opt-in to acted-on recommendation.

elements.name: Recommendation model
elements.notes: Collaborative-filtering model suggesting next actions. Trained on 6 months of usage data. p99 latency <200ms.

meta.xai_strategy: Confidence score shown alongside each recommendation

elements.name: AI ethics review
elements.notes: Internal review covering fairness, explainability, and right-to-opt-out before any user exposure.

elements.name: Recommendation card UI
elements.notes: Card component showing AI suggestion, confidence level, and dismiss option.

elements.name: Model serving infrastructure
elements.notes: Real-time inference endpoint with <200ms p99 latency SLA. Auto-scales on request volume.

elements.name: Explainability gap
elements.notes: Users do not understand why they receive specific recommendations, leading to distrust and low adoption.

elements.name: Bias in training data
elements.notes: Training corpus over-represents power users; recommendations may not generalise to new or low-engagement users.

elements.name: Support agent reviews AI decision log
elements.notes: When a user escalates, support staff access the explanation trail for the AI recommendation that caused confusion.

elements.name: Production model monitor
elements.notes: Tracks prediction drift, feature importance shifts, and data quality. Alerts on-call if drift threshold exceeded.
